title: Apply to a SIT Study Abroad Program for Spring 2027!
summary: Looking for an international study abroad experience that combines rigorous academics with real-world learning? The School for International Training (SIT Study Abroad) offers semester, summer, and academic-year programs across Africa, Asia, Latin America, Europe, and the Middle East. For 2027 , SIT programs provide immersive, field-based learning that allows students to explore critical global issues while earning academic credit. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); SIT programs are designed for students seeking transformative educational experiences that go beyond traditional classroom learning. Participants engage in experiential learning , combining coursework with practical fieldwork, community engagement, and independent research projects. Why Choose SIT SIT Study Abroad is ideal for students who want more than a traditional classroom experience. Programs emphasize intercultural competence, field-based learning, and real-world problem solving , providing skills and experiences that prepare students for careers in international development, environmental science, public health, education, and beyond. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); By combining immersive cultural experiences, academic rigor, and independent research , SIT enables students to develop a global perspective, build professional networks, and gain practical experience that sets them apart in both academic and professional environments. Who Can Apply SIT programs are open to: Undergraduate students enrolled at accredited colleges or universities. Students in good academic standing who meet program-specific prerequisites. Applicants interested in global issues such as sustainability, climate change, human rights, public health, development, and cultural studies . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Each program may have additional requirements, such as language proficiency or specific prior coursework. Students are encouraged to consult the SIT website for detailed program requirements before applying. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Program Structure SIT Study Abroad programs blend academic instruction with hands-on learning , offering: Thematic courses focused on global challenges such as environmental sustainability, social inequality, migration, and health. Field-based learning , including site visits, community engagement, and applied research projects. Independent Study Projects (ISPs) that allow students to explore a topic of personal interest in depth, often involving field research or collaboration with local partners. Opportunities for cultural immersion, including homestays, local mentorship, and language learning. Semester programs typically run for 12 15 weeks, while academic-year programs can extend up to 30 weeks. Summer programs are shorter, offering 6 7 week immersive experiences with intensive learning and fieldwork. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Academic Credit and Support Students earn fully transferable academic credit through SIT s accredited U.S. institution. Academic advising, on-site staff support, and risk management systems ensure that participants have a safe and academically productive experience. SIT also provides guidance on visa applications, health and safety, and travel arrangements. Financial aid and scholarships may be available for eligible students, depending on program and funding cycles. Prospective participants are encouraged to review the website and consult their home institution s study abroad office early in the planning process. Fields of Study SIT programs cover a wide range of thematic areas: Climate and Environment: Programs exploring sustainability, conservation, and climate action in local contexts. Development and Inequality: Addressing social, economic, and political dimensions of development and social justice. Global Health: Studying public health challenges and community-based health initiatives in diverse regions. Peace, Conflict, and Human Rights: Engaging with issues of governance, migration, and human rights protection. Education and Cultural Studies: Examining learning systems, cultural preservation, and cross-cultural engagement. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Each program is grounded in local context, providing students with real-world exposure to regional challenges and opportunities. Application and Deadlines SIT operates on a rolling admissions basis , which means program availability and deadlines vary by location, academic term, and field of study. Popular programs, including Spring 2027 , fill quickly, so early application is strongly recommended. Applicants should: Review program prerequisites carefully. Coordinate with their home institution regarding credit transfer. Prepare academic transcripts, essays, and recommendation letters in advance. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Completing applications early increases the likelihood of placement in preferred programs and access to financial aid. Explore programs and more details here . Looking for more opportunities? Check out the Opportunities for Youth website! The post Apply to a SIT Study Abroad Program for Spring 2027! appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: HIGH IMPACT CLIMATE LEADERSHIP ROLE: Private Equity Stakeholder Project Hiring a Climate Director
summary: The Private Equity Stakeholder Project (PESP) has opened applications for a Climate Director (Remote) a rare senior-level opportunity for experienced climate, environmental justice, and corporate accountability professionals to directly challenge private equity s role in the climate crisis. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); This is a fully remote, full-time leadership position with a competitive salary, strong benefits, and national-level influence across climate advocacy, sustainable finance, and environmental justice movements. If you have deep experience in climate campaigns, financial research, and leading advocacy teams , this role puts you at the center of high-stakes climate accountability work. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the Private Equity Stakeholder Project (PESP) PESP is a nonprofit financial watchdog organization that investigates how private equity firms impact communities, workers, and the environment. The organization focuses on increasing transparency in a sector that often operates behind closed doors especially when it comes to fossil fuel investments, energy infrastructure, and environmental harm . Through rigorous research, data-driven reporting, and coalition-based campaigns , PESP partners with: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Frontline and environmental justice communities Institutional investors Policymakers and regulators Labor unions and advocacy organizations Their work plays a growing role in shaping climate accountability within global finance. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Role Overview: Climate Director (Remote) The Climate Director will lead PESP s Climate Team and oversee all research, strategy, and campaign efforts focused on private equity s climate impact. This role blends: Strategic research leadership Corporate accountability campaigning Team management Coalition and investor engagement Environmental justice advocacy You ll report directly to the Executive Director and help shape national-level climate accountability strategies. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Why This Role Matters Private equity is reshaping the global energy system often quietly, and often in ways that accelerate climate harm . As Climate Director, you will: Confront opaque financial power structures Support communities on the frontlines of fossil fuel investment Influence investor behavior and policy discussions Help align financial systems with climate and justice goals This is not just a job it s a platform for high-impact climate leadership at a moment when accountability in finance has never mattered more. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Strategic Purpose of the Role Private equity firms control vast portfolios in: Fossil fuel extraction Power generation Energy infrastructure Carbon-intensive industries Yet they often face limited public oversight . The Climate Director s mission is to close this accountability gap by: Exposing harmful investment practices Building powerful coalitions Pressuring asset managers toward climate-aligned behavior Supporting frontline communities affected by fossil fuel investments ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Core Responsibilities 1. Climate Accountability Environmental Justice Leadership Lead PESP s corporate accountability work in the energy and fossil fuel sectors Identify pathways for systemic reform in private equity investment practices Center environmental justice and frontline community voices ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); 2. Team Leadership Management Supervise and mentor multiple research and campaign staff Strengthen internal research, advocacy, and data capacity Support professional growth and skill development 3. Research Oversight Data Strategy You will oversee sophisticated research initiatives, including: Managing multi-staff research workflows Developing and maintaining datasets on private equity energy investments Analyzing environmental and climate impact data Producing high-impact public reports for investors, policymakers, and advocates Collaborating across organizations on shared data systems 4. Campaign Strategy Public Engagement Design and execute strategic campaigns targeting private asset managers Set campaign priorities and action plans Coordinate with communications and design teams Edit reports for legal accuracy and strategic clarity Engage with media, policymakers, investors, unions, and grassroots groups ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); 5. Coalition Relationship Building Build partnerships with: Environmental justice organizations Frontline community groups ESG-focused institutional investors Climate and accountability coalitions Translate complex financial research into compelling, accessible narratives 6. Travel Organizational Reporting Report directly to the Executive Director Participate in occasional travel for coalition meetings, campaigns, and stakeholder engagement ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Required Experience Qualifications PESP is seeking senior-level candidates with: 1. Professional Experience 10+ years in strategic research and/or advocacy campaigning Proven leadership experience managing teams Demonstrated success in environmental justice or climate campaigns Experience developing and executing campaign strategies 2. Technical Data Skills Hands-on experience with: Large datasets and data analysis Airtable or SQL (data management) R or Python (analysis) Data visualization tools ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); 3. Communication Advocacy Exceptional written and verbal communication skills Experience engaging: Investors Policymakers Media Labor unions Community-based organizations 4. Values Commitment Strong understanding of financial advocacy within climate movements Deep commitment to social, economic, and climate justice Applications strongly encouraged from women, BIPOC, and LGBTQ+ candidates Salary, Compensation Benefits Salary Range: $105,000 $120,000 USD , based on experience Benefits Include: Medical, dental, and vision insurance 401(k) retirement savings plan Comprehensive employee benefits package Fully remote work structure ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); This compensation reflects the seniority, leadership responsibility, and national impact of the role. How to Apply: Applicants must complete a detailed online application form , including written responses addressing: Motivation for applying Commitment to social, economic, and climate justice Experience in environmental justice campaigns Experience analyzing environmental impact data Leadership and team management experience ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Apply here IMPORTANT: Do NOT use artificial intelligence when writing your application responses or submitting writing samples. PESP explicitly evaluates authentic writing and communication skills. Application Deadline: 6th March 2026 For more information visit here For more global opportunties click here The post HIGH IMPACT CLIMATE LEADERSHIP ROLE: Private Equity Stakeholder Project Hiring a Climate Director appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for DW Akademie Mini Grants for Media Outlets Reporting on Gender Based Violence Online in Syria
summary: If you are a media outlet operating in Syria and passionate about strengthening ethical, gender-sensitive journalism this is one of the most valuable grant opportunities available right now. It offers training, mentorship, and mini-grants of up to 7,000 to support impactful reporting on gender-based violence online and elevate women s voices in media. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); What This Grant Is All About DW Akademie , the international media development arm of Germany s public broadcaster Deutsche Welle, has launched a dedicated Call for Applications aimed at media outlets across Syria. The initiative forms part of the project Strong Female Voices for a United Syria , funded by the German Ausw rtiges Amt (Federal Foreign Office) . This mini-grant scheme is designed to empower media organisations and journalists to: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Implement ethical, gender-sensitive reporting on online gender-based violence (GBV) Develop internal newsroom policies and protective measures for female journalists Build stronger networks for countering disinformation and online violence Receive hands-on support through training and mentorship to elevate quality reporting in challenging environments Grant Value Benefits Selected media outlets will receive: 1. Mini-Grants of up to 7,000 per organisation to implement proposed activities 2. Training Workshops on gender-sensitive reporting and newsroom safety 3. Mentorship and Coaching from experienced media professionals 4. Support developing ethical internal policies for newsroom gender inclusion 5. Opportunity to enhance visibility and strengthen media impact in Syria ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); This funding is not just financial it s a chance to build editorial capacity and amplify under-represented voices through ethical, survivor-centered journalism. Why This Matters Gender-based violence especially in online environments is a pressing issue in the Syrian context, where digital dynamics intersect with longstanding social challenges. Media reporting on GBV not only exposes systemic abuses but also helps shape public understanding and policy responses. This mini-grant programme supports media outlets that are ready to take on that responsibility with professional rigor and social impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Who Can Apply This opportunity welcomes media outlets from across Syria that are committed to journalistic integrity and impactful reporting on issues affecting women and girls in the digital space. Your organisation is encouraged to apply if you: 1. Are a registered media organisation working in print, broadcast, or digital formats 2. Have a track record in producing news, features, or investigative reporting 3. Are focused on issues including gender equality, digital safety, media ethics, or human rights 4. Are ready to implement concrete activities and reporting plans on gender-based violence online How to Apply Submission Requirements To apply, media outlets should prepare and submit a comprehensive application that typically includes: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Organisation Profile: Background, mission, and editorial focus Project Proposal: Clear description of planned activities to report on gender-based violence online, including goals, methods, timelines, and impact expectations Budget Plan: Justified use of the 7,000 or less grant for project implementation Team Information: Bios of key staff who will execute the project ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Contact Details: Primary contact person for communication and follow-up Apply here Ensure your submission is complete before the deadline late or partial applications will not be considered. Key Deadlines Timeline Application Deadline: 28th February Apply for DW Akademie Mini Grants for Media Outlets Reporting on Gender Based Violence Online in Syria2026 applications must be submitted by this date to be considered ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Award Announcements: Shortly after review Implementation Period: Following selection, grant activities and project implementation will begin in early 2026 Tips for a Strong Application Clearly define the scope and impact of your reporting plan Highlight how your outlet will implement ethical reporting standards Demonstrate your editorial capacity and organisational readiness to deliver Include gender safety measures for journalists in your strategy Show how your project contributes to long-term newsroom improvements This grant opportunity presents a significant boost for Syrian media outlets committed to ethical journalism, gender justice, and digital safety, with 7,000 in support, expert guidance, and lasting newsroom impact. Don t miss this chance to elevate your reporting, strengthen your organisation s policies, and contribute meaningfully to transforming media narratives around gender-based violence. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Apply for DW Akademie Mini Grants for Media Outlets Reporting on Gender Based Violence Online in Syria appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
